slug: 004-案例分享：案外人误转资金至被执行人账户，能否排除执行？

zh.title: 案例分享：案外人误转资金至被执行人账户，能否排除执行？
zh.lead: 在民事执行程序中，执行程序中，案外人因操作失误将款项转入被执行人账户的情形并不少见。此类纠纷的争议焦点在于：“误转”款项是否属于被执行人责任财产？案外人能否通过执行异议程序排除执行？本文结合本所律师代理的一个成功回款的执行案件，解析此类案件的法律规则及实务要点。

blocks.text: 案情简介：50万元货款误转被执行人账户，案外人异议被驳回
在王先生与施某买卖合同纠纷一案中，施某未履行生效法律文书确定的支付义务，于是王先生委托本所律师代为提起强制执行申请。
在执行过程中，施某名下某银行的存款账户被法院冻结。案外人詹某、陈某因与施某之子施小某有业务往来，需支付货款，2025年1月，詹某委托其妻陈某向施小某账户转账50万元，于是，陈某向施某账户转账50万元，附言“灵芝粉款”，意图让施某代施小某收款。因施某账户被冻结，款项无法转出，交易中止。詹某、陈某以转账错误为由，向法院提出执行异议，要求原路退回50万元货款。
争议焦点：
误转款项是否仍属施某责任财产？
案外人能否以“误转”为由排除执行？
律师直击要害：从货币权属到证据规则，三大抗辩筑牢执行防线
货币的“占有即所有”规则——涉案款项所有权已发生转移，属于被执行人施某的可供执行的财产
根据《最高人民法院关于人民法院办理执行异议和复议案件若干问题的规定》第二十五条第一款第（三）项，银行存款的权属以账户登记名称为准。货币作为种类物，遵循“占有即所有”原则，一旦转入被执行人账户即视为其责任财产，法院可依法执行。
误转行为的证明责任——案外人詹某和陈某夫妇提供的证据不足以证明此次转款是误转
陈某明知转入账户为施某所有，其提供的银行账户交易记录仅仅能证明存在转账行为，但无法证明系误转，陈荣平在明知收款方为施深根却仍选择转账的情况下，自称此次转账行为系“误转”，并不符合常理，且转账附言“灵芝粉款”未明确代收性质，故案涉50万元归属施某，属于可执行财产。
法院裁判核心：为何“误转”主张无法阻却执行？

法院认为，《最高人民法院关于人民法院办理执行异议和复议案件若干问题的规定》第二十五条第一款第三项的规定，“案外人对人民法院强制执行的银行存款提出执行异议的，人民法院应当按照金融机构登记的账户名称判断执行异议人是否系权利人。”本案中，被执行人施某有未履行完毕的执行案件，法院冻结被执行人施某的存款账户符合法律规定。案外人詹某、陈某所称的汇入被执行人施某的存款账户内的50万元货款，其在转账前已知晓汇入的账户为被执行人名下银行账户，该行为并不属于错汇、误汇，其主张该款归其所有，无法律依据，法院不予支持。据此，法院驳回了案外人詹某、陈某的异议请求。
执行效果：最终，该50万元货款全部用于执行，王先生债权全额实现！本案于2025年2月执行结案。
律师实务指南：如何防范误转风险？遭遇执行异议该如何应对？
误转资金的救济路径
执行异议程序
该程序仅适用于对执行标的享有物权或特殊债权的案外人，因本案中资金进入施某账户后已与其他款项混同，不具有特定化，在事实上无法返还“原物”，无权主张“原物返还”。普通债权（如本案不当得利）无法排除执行。
另诉主张不当得利
根据《最高人民法院关于适用执行程序若干问题的解释》第十四条之规定，詹某、陈某以误转的不当得利请求之债权请求为由，无法请求排除以物权为基础的强制执行行为。
案外人可另案起诉被执行人要求返还款项，但需与其他债权人平等受偿，无优先性。
特殊情形突破“占有即所有”的可能性
若误转款项满足以下条件，案外人可能排除执行：
专款专用账户：如农民工工资专户、保证金账户等，资金具有特定用途且未混同；
账户被特定化：款项进入账户后未被使用，且与被执行人其他资金区分；
明确代收合意：案外人与被执行人签订代收协议，且第三人（实际收款方）确认代收事实。
风险防范建议
转账前双重确认：核对账户名称、账号、开户行，避免仅凭“亲属关系”或“历史交易”盲目转账。
备注明确用途：如为代收代付，应在转账附言中注明“代XX收款”，并留存书面协议、聊天记录等证据。
及时采取保全措施：发现误转后立即报警、申请账户冻结，防止款项被划扣。

references: 《中华人民共和国民事诉讼法》
《最高人民法院关于适用<中华人民共和国民事诉讼法>执行程序若干问题的解释》
《最高人民法院关于人民法院办理执行异议和复议案件若干问题的规定》
zh.conclusion:
误转款项能否排除执行，核心在于资金权属认定及案外人权利性质。货币的“占有即所有”规则具有普适性，案外人仅凭“误转”主张排除执行难获支持。企业和个人在交易中应重视账户合规管理，发生纠纷时及时寻求专业法律帮助，通过精准的法律策略维护权益。
关注我们，获取更多执行异议与救济实务指南！
（本文案例及观点供实务参考，具体案件需结合管辖法院规则分析。）

en.title: Case Sharing: Can a Third Party Who Mistakenly Transfers Funds to the Account of the Execution Debtor Exclude Enforcement?
en.lead: In civil enforcement proceedings, it is not uncommon for an outsider to mistakenly transfer funds into the account of the judgment debtor due to operational errors. The focus of disputes in such cases is: whether the mistakenly transferred funds constitute the judgment debtor's liable property? Can the outsider exclude enforcement through the enforcement objection procedure? This article, based on an enforcement case in which our firm's lawyers successfully recovered funds, analyzes the legal rules and practical points of such cases.
en.conclusion:
Whether mistakenly transferred funds can be excluded from enforcement hinges on the determination of fund ownership and the nature of the third party's rights. The rule that "possession is equivalent to ownership" of currency is universally applicable, and a third party asserting solely a "mistaken transfer" to oppose enforcement is unlikely to prevail. Enterprises and individuals should prioritize account compliance management in transactions, and promptly seek professional legal assistance upon the occurrence of disputes to safeguard their rights through precise legal strategies.  
Follow us for more practical guidance on enforcement objections and remedies!  
(The case and viewpoints in this article are for practical reference. Specific cases require analysis in accordance with the rules of the competent court.)
references: Civil Code of the People's Republic of China
Civil Procedure Law of the People's Republic of China
Interpretation of the Supreme People's Court on Several Issues concerning the Application of the Enforcement Procedures in the Civil Procedure Law of the People's Republic of China
Provisions of the Supreme People's Court on Several Issues Concerning the Handling of Enforcement Objection and Reconsideration Cases by People's Courts

blocks.text: Case Summary: 500,000 yuan of payment mistakenly transferred to the account of the person subject to enforcement; objection by a third party dismissed.

In the case of the dispute over a sales contract between Mr. Wang and Shi Mou, Shi Mou failed to fulfill the payment obligation as determined by the effective legal instrument. Therefore, Mr. Wang entrusted a lawyer from our firm to file an application for compulsory enforcement on his behalf.

During the enforcement proceedings, a deposit account held by Shi under a certain bank was frozen by the court. Non-parties Zhan and Chen, due to business dealings with Shi’s son Shi Xiaoxiao, needed to pay for goods. In January 2025, Zhan instructed his wife Chen to transfer RMB 500,000 to Shi Xiaoxiao’s account. Consequently, Chen transferred RMB 500,000 to Shi’s account with the note “payment for Ganoderma lucidum powder,” intending for Shi to collect the payment on behalf of Shi Xiaoxiao. Because Shi’s account was frozen, the funds could not be transferred out, and the transaction was suspended. Zhan and Chen, on the grounds of a mistaken transfer, filed an objection to enforcement with the court, requesting that the RMB 500,000 payment be returned via the original route.
Issues in Dispute:
Do the mistakenly transferred funds still constitute Shi's liable property?
Can a third party exclude enforcement on the grounds of "mistaken transfer"?
Attorney strikes at the heart: from currency ownership to rules of evidence, three defenses fortify the enforcement defense line.

The rule of "possession is ownership" for currency — the ownership of the involved funds has been transferred, and it constitutes property of the judgment debtor Shi that is available for enforcement.

According to Article 25, Paragraph 1, Item (3) of the Provisions of the Supreme People's Court on Several Issues Concerning the Handling of Enforcement Objections and Reconsideration Cases by People's Courts, the ownership of bank deposits shall be determined by the name registered in the account. Currency, as a fungible thing, follows the principle of "possession is ownership." Once transferred into the account of the person subject to enforcement, it is deemed as his/her liable property, and the court may enforce it in accordance with the law.
Burden of proof for mistaken transfer — The evidence provided by non-party Zhan and his spouse Chen is insufficient to prove that this transfer was a mistaken transfer.

Chen Mou was aware that the account into which the transfer was made belonged to Shi Mou. The bank account transaction records provided could only prove the existence of a transfer but could not demonstrate that it was a mistaken transfer. Under circumstances where Chen Rongping knew that the payee was Shi Shengen yet still chose to initiate the transfer, claiming that the transfer was a "mistaken transfer" is inconsistent with common sense. Moreover, the transfer memo "payment for Lingzhi powder" did not explicitly indicate the nature of the collection on behalf of another. Therefore, the 500,000 yuan involved in the case belongs to Shi Mou and constitutes enforceable property.
Core of Court's Ruling: Why Cannot the "Mistaken Transfer" Claim Preclude Enforcement?

The court held that, according to Article 25, Paragraph 1, Item 3 of the Provisions of the Supreme People's Court on Several Issues concerning the Handling of Execution Objections and Reconsideration Cases by the People's Courts, "where a person other than the party concerned raises an execution objection to a bank deposit that is subject to compulsory enforcement by the people's court, the people's court shall determine whether the objector is the right holder based on the account name recorded by the financial institution." In this case, the person subject to enforcement, Shi Mou, had an execution case that was not fully performed, and the court's freezing of the deposit account of the person subject to enforcement, Shi Mou, was in compliance with legal provisions. The 500,000 yuan payment for goods that the persons other than the parties concerned, Zhan Mou and Chen Mou, claimed to have been remitted into the deposit account of the person subject to enforcement, Shi Mou, was made with their prior knowledge that the account into which they remitted was a bank account in the name of the person subject to enforcement; this act does not constitute a mistaken remittance or erroneous remittance. Their claim that the money belonged to them lacks legal basis and is not supported by the court. Accordingly, the court rejected the objection requests of the persons other than the parties concerned, Zhan Mou and Chen Mou.

Enforcement effect: Ultimately, the entire RMB 500,000 payment was fully applied to enforcement, and Mr. Wang's creditor's rights were fully realized! This case was closed by enforcement in February 2025.
Lawyer's Practice Guide: How to Prevent the Risk of Wrongful Transfer? How to Respond to Enforcement Objections?
Remedy Path for Erroneous Transfer of Funds
Enforcement Objection Procedure

This procedure applies only to third parties who have real rights or special creditor's rights in the subject matter of enforcement. Since the funds in this case, upon entering Shi's account, have been commingled with other funds and lack specificity, the "original property" cannot be returned in fact, and the claim for "return of the original property" is not warranted. Ordinary creditor's rights (such as unjust enrichment in this case) cannot preclude enforcement.
Claim for unjust enrichment in a separate lawsuit

According to Article 14 of the Interpretation of the Supreme People's Court on Several Issues concerning the Application of Enforcement Procedures, Zhan and Chen, on the grounds of their claim for creditor's rights arising from unjust enrichment due to mistaken transfer, cannot request to exclude the enforcement action based on property rights.
A third party may file a separate lawsuit against the judgment debtor to seek the return of funds, but shall share equally with other creditors in the distribution of proceeds, without priority.
The Possibility of Breaking Through the "Possession Is Ownership" Principle in Special Circumstances
If the mistakenly transferred funds satisfy the following conditions, a third party may preclude enforcement:
Special-purpose account: such as special accounts for migrant workers' wages, security deposit accounts, etc., where funds have specific purposes and are not commingled.
The account is specified: the funds have not been used after entering the account and are distinguished from other funds of the judgment debtor;

Explicit agreement for collection on behalf: A third party outside the case and the judgment debtor have entered into a collection agreement, and the third party (the actual payee) confirms the fact of collection on behalf.
Risk Prevention Recommendations
Double confirmation before transfer: verify the account name, account number, and account opening bank; avoid blind transfers based solely on "kinship" or "historical transactions."

Note: Clearly specify the purpose: if it is for collection and payment on behalf, indicate "receiving on behalf of XX" in the transfer remarks, and retain written agreements, chat records, and other evidence.
Promptly take preservation measures: upon discovering an erroneous transfer, immediately report to the police and apply for account freezing to prevent the funds from being transferred out.
